recipes.title: Thieboudienne
recipes.description: Senegal's national dish: red rice cooked in a tomato fish broth with stuffed fish and root vegetables.

ingredients.name: white fish steaks (grouper or sea bass)

steps: Pound parsley, garlic, salt, and a piece of scotch bonnet to a paste (rof).
Slit the fish steaks and stuff with the rof. Marinate for 15 minutes.
Heat oil in a heavy pot and brown the fish on both sides, 3 minutes per side. Remove.
Sauté onions in the same oil for 5 minutes.
Add tomato paste and fry for 6 minutes until darkened.
Add chopped tomatoes, yet, and 1.5 l water. Bring to a boil.
Add carrots, cassava, cabbage, okra, and remaining scotch bonnet. Simmer 25 minutes.
Return the fish to the pot for 8 minutes, then remove fish and vegetables to a platter.
Add the rinsed rice to the broth (it should just be covered). Cook covered on low heat 25 minutes.
Mound the rice on a large platter, arrange fish and vegetables on top, and serve.

recipes.title: Yassa
recipes.description: Senegalese chicken or fish marinated in lemon and onion, then simmered in caramelized onion sauce with mustard.
steps: Combine chicken with sliced onions, lemon juice, mustard, garlic, scotch bonnet, salt, and pepper. Marinate at least 4 hours.
Lift chicken from the marinade and pat dry.
Sear the chicken in oil over high heat for 4 minutes per side until golden. Remove.
Drain the onions and add them to the pot. Cook 25 minutes over medium-low until caramelized.
Stir in the marinade liquid, bay leaves, and stock cubes.
Return chicken to the pot, add water to come halfway up the chicken.
Cover and simmer 35 minutes, turning chicken once.
Stir in the extra mustard at the end and reduce 5 minutes.
Serve over white rice.

recipes.title: Mafé
recipes.description: West African groundnut stew of beef or lamb in a rich peanut and tomato sauce with root vegetables.
steps: Brown the beef in oil over high heat for 6 minutes, then remove.
Sauté onion and garlic 5 minutes.
Add tomato paste and fry 5 minutes until darkened.
Add blended tomatoes and cook 8 minutes.
Whisk peanut butter with 300 ml hot water and add to the pot.
Return beef, add stock cubes, scotch bonnet, salt, and remaining water.
Cover and simmer 60 minutes, stirring occasionally so it doesn't stick.
Add carrots, sweet potato, and cabbage. Cook another 25 minutes until vegetables are tender and sauce is glossy.
Adjust salt and serve over white rice or with rice balls.

recipes.title: Pastels
recipes.description: Senegalese fried fish turnovers with a spiced fish filling, served with onion-tomato dipping sauce.

ingredients.name: white fish fillet, cooked and flaked

steps: Make the dough: rub butter into flour and salt, add egg and water, knead, and rest 30 minutes.
For the filling: sauté onion, garlic, and scotch bonnet in 2 tbsp oil for 4 minutes.
Add tomato paste and stock cube, fry 3 minutes.
Stir in the flaked fish, parsley, and salt. Cook 4 minutes and cool.
Roll the dough thin and cut into 10 cm circles.
Place a tablespoon of filling on each, fold over, and crimp the edges.
Heat oil to 170°C and fry pastels in batches for 4 minutes per side until golden.
Drain on paper towels.
Serve hot with onion-tomato kaani sauce.

recipes.title: Caldou
recipes.description: Casamance-style Senegalese poached fish in a tangy tomato-lemon broth, served over white rice.
steps: Marinate the fish steaks in half the lemon juice, garlic, parsley, and salt for 20 minutes.
Heat oil and sear the fish 2 minutes per side. Remove.
In the same pot, sauté onion 5 minutes.
Add the blended tomatoes and cook 10 minutes until pulpy.
Pour in water with stock cube and scotch bonnet. Simmer 8 minutes.
Add okra and the remaining lemon juice; simmer 5 minutes.
Return the fish to the pot and poach 8 minutes until just cooked.
Adjust salt and serve over white rice.

recipes.title: Thiakry
recipes.description: Senegalese chilled millet couscous dessert with sweetened yogurt, vanilla, and raisins.
steps: Steam the millet couscous over simmering water for 20 minutes until tender.
Fluff with a fork and let cool completely.
In a bowl, whisk yogurt, sour cream, condensed milk, sugar, vanilla, and nutmeg until smooth.
Fold the cooled couscous into the yogurt mixture.
Stir in the raisins.
Chill for at least 2 hours.
Serve cold in bowls or cups.

recipes.title: Dibi
recipes.description: Senegalese street-style grilled lamb or mutton, charred over wood and served with sliced onions and mustard.

ingredients.name: boneless lamb shoulder, in 3 cm chunks

steps: Mix lamb with mustard, lemon juice, garlic, oil, pepper, stock cube, and salt. Marinate 2 hours.
Prepare a hot wood or charcoal grill.
Thread lamb onto skewers or place chunks directly on the grate.
Grill over high heat for 4 minutes per side, turning to char all surfaces, until just blushing inside.
Meanwhile, soak the onion slices in salted water 5 minutes, drain, and squeeze.
Toss the onions with extra mustard and a slit scotch bonnet.
Pile the grilled lamb onto a plate with the onions on top.
Serve with bread or rice.

recipes.title: Bissap
recipes.description: Vibrant Senegalese drink of dried hibiscus calyces brewed with sugar and a hint of mint or vanilla, served chilled.
steps: Rinse the hibiscus flowers under cold water.
Bring water to a boil in a large pot.
Add the hibiscus and reduce heat. Simmer 15 minutes.
Turn off heat, add the mint sprigs, and steep covered for 20 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine sieve into a pitcher, pressing the flowers.
While still warm, stir in sugar, vanilla, and lemon juice until dissolved.
Cool completely and refrigerate.
Serve over ice.

recipes.title: Ngalakh
recipes.description: Senegalese sweet millet pudding made for Eid, blended with peanut butter, baobab fruit, and raisins.
steps: Steam the millet flour in a couscous steamer over simmering water for 20 minutes, then fluff and cool.
Whisk baobab powder with 500 ml water until smooth, then strain.
Pour the baobab water into a pot with another 500 ml water and bring to a simmer.
Whisk in the peanut butter, sugar, vanilla, nutmeg, and salt until smooth.
Cook 5 minutes, stirring, until creamy.
Stir the cooled steamed millet into the sauce.
Add raisins and simmer 5 more minutes.
Cool, then refrigerate at least 2 hours and serve cold.
